topic.name: Student Loan Regulations
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative amendments and regulations related to student loans and financial aid in the higher education sector.

Document 3: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to set the interest rate on Direct Loans.
Requires any savings to the federal government that result from this Act's amendment to be used for deficit reduction.
Document 4: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to repeal the Secretary of Education's authority under the Direct Loan program.

Document 5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require private educational lenders to submit to the Secretary of Education information regarding each private education loan they make.
Requires the private education loan information to include the total amount and type of each loan.
Document 6: Directs the Department of Education to establish a program to refinance the outstanding principal, interest, and late charges on Federal Direct Loans.
Eliminates the origination fee on Direct Subsidized, Unsubsidized, and PLUS Loans.
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a private student loan lender to disclose clearly and conspicuously a cosigner's obligations.
Prohibits adverse actions against a cosigner for a borrower's death, disability, or bankruptcy.
Document 8: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require that when the holder of a borrower's private education loans receives a prepayment, it is to be applied first toward any fees due on such loans.
Makes that requirement inapplicable to income-based repayment plans for FFELs or DLs.
Document 9: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to increase the maximum annual and aggregate unsubsidized Stafford loan amounts.
Permits the Secretary to act in such capacity after such date if economic conditions threaten timely, uninterrupted access to student loans.
Document 10: Makes procedures for the enforcement of arbitration agreements inapplicable to an enrollment agreement between a student and an institution of higher education.
Forbids an institution from requiring a student to agree to any restriction on the student's ability to pursue a claim against it in court.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative proposals aimed at reforming campaign finance laws in the United States to enhance transparency, reduce the influence of money in politics, and promote fair elections.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements and benefits of fair elections financing of Senate election campaigns.
Establishment of a Fair Elections Fund, eligibility for Fund allocations, contribution and expenditure requirements.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to treat as a campaign contribution any payment made by any person for a coordinated expenditure.
Prescribes penalties for knowing and willful violation of this Act by a contribution which consists of a payment for a coordinated expenditure.
Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to require U.S. entities to obtain and have an employer identification number (EIN) assigned by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS).

The IRS may disclose to federal law enforcement officials taxpayer identify information, including an EIN and information from an application for an EIN, for use in investigations and prosecutions of specified offenses related to money laundering and supporting or financing terrorism.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a refundable credit of up to $25 for qualified grassroots federal congressional campaign contributions paid by the taxpayer.

Directs the Grassroots Democracy Advisory Commission (GDAC) to establish a pilot program under which a qualified individual shall be provided with a 'Grassroots Democracy Coupon' during the election cycle.
Document 5: Amends the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 (LDA) with respect to lobbying disclosures reports, including electronic filing and mandatory free availability to the public over the Internet.
Requires public disclosure by Members of Congress of employment negotiations.
Document 6: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to public financing of presidential election campaigns to quadruple the matching rate for contributions to primary election candidates.
Prohibits political parties from spending unregulated funds (soft money) on their national conventions.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to impose restrictions on foreign jurisdictions or financial institutions operating in the United States that are of prime money laundering concern.
Impose additional requirements for third party summonses used to obtain information in tax investigations.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code and the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the system of public financing for presidential primary and general elections.
Increases the amount of matching funds for presidential primaries from a one-to-one match to a five-to-one match for contributions of $250 or less from individuals.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require the treasurer of each authorized political committee to establish a separate account for the committee consisting exclusively of contributions received by or on behalf of it.
Prohibits the use of amounts in such account to make disbursements by or on behalf of the committee.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements, seed money limits, and benefits of clean election financing of House election campaigns.
Establishes the Clean Elections Review Commission.
